name: Data Visualizer
description: Creates charts and visualizations from JSON data. Produces images (PNG) or HTML with embedded charts.
limitations: limited to standard chart types (bar, line, scatter, pie)
code_template:
import matplotlib matplotlib.use('Agg') import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import io import base64 def data_visualizer(data: dict, chart_type: str = "bar", title: str = "Chart", x_field: str = "", y_field: str = "") -> dict: records = data.get("data", []) if not records: return {"error": "No data to visualize"} x_values = [r.get(x_field, i) for i, r in enumerate(records)] y_values = [r.get(y_field, 0) for r in records] fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6)) if chart_type == "bar": ax.bar(range(len(x_values)), y_values, tick_label=[str(x) for x in x_values]) elif chart_type == "line": ax.plot(x_values, y_values) elif chart_type == "scatter": ax.scatter(x_values, y_values) ax.set_title(title) ax.set_xlabel(x_field) ax.set_ylabel(y_field) buf = io.BytesIO() fig.savefig(buf, format='png', bbox_inches='tight') plt.close(fig) img_base64 = base64.b64encode(buf.getvalue()).decode() html = f'

{title}

' return {"html": html, "image_base64": img_base64}
